title: DCA Accumulator
description: Dollar-cost averaging into BTC/ETH. Scheduled leg fires weekly (Mon 09:00 local by default); dip-acceleration leg fires immediately on a >8% 24h drop, capped at 3/month/asset. All fires are simulation=true until SIMULATION_MODE is flipped globally.

panels.title: Hours since last scheduled BTC fire
panels.description: Weekly cadence — should sit under ~168h. Anything above 200h means a missed Monday (TZ bug, container crash over the weekend, config disabled).

panels.title: Hours since last scheduled ETH fire
Minutes since last cycle
panels.description: The DCA loop runs every hour. If this climbs past ~75m, the container is stuck or crashed.

panels.title: Portfolio value (sizing basis)
panels.description: From Redis key portfolio:estimated_value. Defaults to $10,000 when unset — if you see exactly that, the upstream feed isn't writing.

panels.title: Dip-acceleration fires (30d)
panels.description: Count of extra buys triggered by >8% 24h drops. Zero is normal in calm markets; consistent non-zero means you're catching volatility.

panels.title: Capital deployed (30d)
panels.description: Sum of all DCA buy notionals over the last 30 days — scheduled + dip, both symbols.

panels.title: CoinGecko fetch failures (24h)
panels.description: Each failure blocks the dip check for that symbol/cycle. Persistent failures mean we can't act on drawdowns — fix the API key or rate limit.

panels.title: Cumulative capital deployed by symbol
panels.description: Running total of USD committed to each asset. Slope = average weekly allocation; steps = dip-acceleration events.

panels.title: Fires per week by reason
panels.description: Weekly count of buys split by scheduled vs. dip-acceleration. Healthy pattern: 2 scheduled/week (one per asset), dips as bonus.

panels.title: Accelerations used this month
panels.description: Cap is 3/month/asset (configurable via dca_accumulator.dip_acceleration.max_accelerations). Hitting the cap silently blocks further dip buys until month rollover.

options.content:
Every hourly cycle, the executor asks CoinGecko for the 24h price change of BTC and ETH. If either is below `trigger_drop` (default **-8%**), it publishes a DCA signal sized at `multiplier × base_amount` (default **2×**), then increments `dca:accelerations:{symbol}:{YYYY-MM}` in Redis.

- **Cap**: 3 fires/month/asset (`max_accelerations`). Beyond the cap, the function logs and returns — no error, no alert.
- **Reset**: Redis key expires after ~5 weeks; the gauge snaps back at month rollover.
- **Failure mode**: if CoinGecko 24h fetch fails, the symbol is silently skipped for that cycle — see the 'CoinGecko fetch failures' stat at the top.

panels.title: CLI: read recent DCA signals from Redis

options.content:
The email digest reads `signals:dca:queue` — the same list Prometheus counts above. To view the raw JSON of the most recent fires:

```bash
# Last 10 DCA signals, prettified
docker compose exec redis redis-cli LRANGE signals:dca:queue 0 9 \
  | docker compose exec -T polymarket jq -s '.'

# Current acceleration counter per asset (keys expire ~5 weeks after month end)
docker compose exec redis redis-cli --scan --pattern 'dca:accelerations:*'

# This week's scheduled-fire marker (prevents double-fire within the 30min window)
docker compose exec redis redis-cli --scan --pattern 'dca:last_scheduled:*'
```

Remember: every signal object has `"simulation": true` as long as `SIMULATION_MODE=true` in the `.env`. The defi_executor consumes these signals in live mode; in sim it's log-only.
